name: session-upload-workflow
description: Required workflow for uploading/exporting a session as an agent-session template.
reason: User defined the PR-based upload workflow and canonical TEMPLATES.md index format for session templates.
content:
Context file: session-upload-workflow
Description: Required workflow for uploading/exporting a session as an agent-session template.
Last updated by: opencode-agent
Reason: User defined the PR-based upload workflow and canonical TEMPLATES.md index format for session templates.

<session-upload-workflow>
When the user asks to upload/export a session template, follow this workflow unless the user explicitly overrides it. Upload is more involved than install because it writes to the GitHub repository and should go through PR review.

Source session selection:
- First ask the user for the concrete source sessionID, unless they already provided it.
- If the user does not know the sessionID, tell them to ask that session what its id is.
- If the user still does not know, use the opencode HTTP API session list to query relevant session information and help identify the source session.
- Do not routinely scan Global.Path.data/session/*/metadata.json just to discover candidate sessions.
- Do not query the SQLite database for routine session discovery. Use the HTTP API if discovery is needed.

Upload prerequisites:
- Check that local git is installed before uploading.
- Check that GitHub CLI/auth or another available GitHub authentication mechanism is present for pushing branches and creating PRs. Prefer gh when available.
- Check that the default or user-supplied repository is reachable and that the authenticated account can push a branch.
- These checks are only required for upload/export PR workflows, not for installing templates from the remote repository.

Manager nickname:
- Before committing, check whether the manager session has a persisted nickname context file. Use this nickname to identify the contributor/updater in commit messages, template README changelogs, and TEMPLATES.md.
- Suggested file: context/manager-nickname.json with assemble true and a <manager-nickname> block containing the nickname.
- If no nickname file exists, ask the user to choose a short nickname, then persist it before continuing.

Repository and package defaults:
- Use the default agent repository from the default-agent-repository context unless the user explicitly provides another repository URL or local repository path.
- Use manage-session export for upload/export operations. Pass repositoryUrl with the default repository when the user did not provide a repository.
- Before exporting, inspect only the selected source session's metadata.json and existing context/system/tool files.
- Export only managed session package files supported by manage-session: context/, system/, tool/, assemble.ts, and assemble-schema.md. Do not export histories, messages, database records, auth files, or provider credentials.

Template README and versioning:
- Every uploaded template folder must contain README.md describing the session template and its version.
- For a new template, create README.md with template name, version, description, intended use, included managed files, updater nickname, date, and changelog.
- For updates to an existing template, compare against the repository version, update README.md with the new version and a changelog summary of the changes.
- Use semantic versioning unless the user specifies another version. Ask when the version bump is ambiguous.

Repository template index:
- The repository uses a single Markdown metadata document, TEMPLATES.md, as the canonical human-readable index for all current session templates in the repository. Do not maintain a parallel JSON index unless the user later changes this policy.
- TEMPLATES.md is an index of the latest metadata for every template, not a changelog. Detailed changelogs belong in each template folder's README.md.
- Every upload must create or update TEMPLATES.md so it includes one row per current template in the repository. Existing templates not touched by the upload must remain listed.
- Each row should describe the template's latest state after the upload: template name, repository path, current version, short description, updated date, and updater nickname.
- Use this table format unless the user explicitly changes it:

# Agent Session Templates

This file is the canonical human-readable index for session templates in this repository.

| Template | Path | Version | Description | Updated | Updater |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `template-name` | `agents/template-name` | `0.1.0` | Short description. | YYYY-MM-DD | `nickname` |

- Keep template rows stable and parseable: use backticks around Template, Path, and Updater; keep dates as YYYY-MM-DD; keep Description to one concise sentence.
- Installation workflow should use TEMPLATES.md as the repository-level template index once it exists; if it does not exist, fall back to scanning agents/*/agent.json.

PR workflow:
- Do not push template changes directly to main for normal uploads.
- Create a branch for the template upload/update, commit the exported files plus README.md and TEMPLATES.md, push the branch, and create a PR targeting main.
- Branch naming should be concise and include the template name, version, and nickname when practical.
- The agent should automate README updates, TEMPLATES.md updates, branch creation, commit, push, and PR creation whenever credentials and tools allow.
- If automatic PR creation fails after pushing the branch, report the branch and provide the manual PR URL or instructions.
- Do not merge the PR unless the user explicitly asks and the current account has permission. Normal review/merge is the responsibility of a qualified repository manager.

Safety and reporting:
- If the target package name or package path is ambiguous, ask the user before exporting.
- If updating an existing template, show the detected existing package and ask for confirmation before proceeding.
- Report the source session id, repository, package path, version, updater nickname, exported files, branch name, and PR URL if created.
- Explicitly state that the template update is not available on main until a qualified repository manager reviews and merges the PR.
</session-upload-workflow>
